news.headline: Korea Zinc's AGM on March 28, 2025 showed current management maintaining board control with strong shareholder support.
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, in Seoul, South Korea, Korea Zinc (KRX:010130) revealed the results of its annual general meeting (AGM), confirming that the existing management has maintained control of the board amidst challenges from competitors MBK and Young Poong. news.summary: After eight weeks of treatment, clinical remission was observed in 33% of patients who received PL8177, compared to 0% in the placebo group. The study also showed a statistically significant clinical response in 78% of PL8177-treated patients, in contrast to 33% in the placebo group (p<0.005). The symptomatic...

news.summary: Initial effectiveness data from the Phase 1/2 dose expansion cohort reveals long-lasting responses and reduction in tumor size among a group of patients with a "hot" tumor resistant to PD-(L)1.

news.summary: Lexicon has the opportunity to receive a total of $1 billion in payments, which include $75 million in upfront and near-term milestone payments. This financial support encompasses upfront, development, regulatory, and sales milestone payments.

Humacyte's Q4 and 2024 year-end financial results revealed; FDA approved Symvess™ for vascular trauma treatment.
news.summary: Symvess™, an acellular tissue-engineered vessel-tyod, has recently been approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for the treatment of extremity vascular trauma. This approval marks a significant advancement in medical technology, providing a new treatment option for patients with this type of injury. The approval process involved rigorous testing and evaluation to ensure the safety and efficacy of Symvess™. This new treatment option has the potential to benefit a substantial number of individuals who suffer from extremity vascular trauma, offering hope for improved outcomes and quality of life.

news.headline: Super League reported Q4 and full year 2024 financial results, focusing on path to profitability and revenue diversification. Ann Hand becomes Executive Chair; Matt Edelman named CEO.
news.summary: Ann Hand will be assuming the role of Executive Chair to lead strategic alternative initiatives, as President Matt Edelman is set to be promoted as the new Chief Executive Officer. This move signifies a shift in the company's leadership structure, with Hand taking on a key position in driving strategic decisions. Edelman's promotion reflects his success in his previous role and showcases the company's commitment to internal talent development. The changes are expected to bring fresh perspectives to the organization and drive growth.

news.headline: China to conduct review on CK Hutch port deal - Sources
Aave partners with Chainlink SVR to increase revenue by recapturing liquidation MEV. Major move in DeFi.
news.summary: SVR has successfully recovered a significant amount of previously leaked MEV from blockchains, amounting to a historical sum. This reclaimed liquidity has now been redirected back to DeFi applications, aiding in the generation of fresh revenue streams. The accomplishment was made public in San Francisco on March 28, 2025. The collaboration involved Chainlink, a renowned platform for onchain finance, and BGD Labs, an initiative within the Web3 sector with a strong emphasis on Aave. This initiative marks a significant milestone in the utilization of blockchain technology for financial purposes.

news.headline: India aims to diversify lithium imports with stake in SQM's Australian projects. A 20% stake worth $600 million sought by Indian consortium led by KABIL to cater to growing market.
news.summary: India is making a bid to acquire a stake in Chilean mining giant SQM's (NYSE: SQM) ventures in Australia in a move to gain access to essential battery minerals as its demand for lithium grows. Reports from undisclosed sources revealed that an Indian consortium, spearheaded by Khanij Bidesh India Ltd (KABIL), is eyeing stakes in SQM's Mount Holland and Andover lithium projects in Western Australia. The consortium is considering a 20% stake in these projects, valued at approximately $600 million. This marks India's most significant effort to secure overseas lithium supplies, with negotiations ongoing and companies showing interest through initial offers. India's interest in SQM's projects signifies its pursuit to boost its lithium reserves to cater to its burgeoning market for electric vehicles.

news.headline: Halal cosmetics market to reach US$135.54 billion by 2033, growing at a compound annual rate of 12.95% from 2025-2033.
news.summary: An analysis report titled "Halal Cosmetics Market Size and Share Analysis - Growth Trends and Forecast Report 2025-2033" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's available reports. The Halal Cosmetics market is projected to increase significantly, reaching a value of US$ 135.54 billion by 2033. This is a notable growth from the market's value of US$ 45.30 billion in 2024, representing a compound annual growth rate of 12.95% expected between 2025 and 2033. The surge in market value is attributed to rising consumer demand, increased awareness of ethical standards, and a continuously expanding market.

news.headline: Karan Brar, Sophie Reynolds, and Mediaplanet work to raise awareness for the millions affected by epilepsy.
news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that millions of individuals around the globe are affected by epilepsy, a condition characterized by unpredictable seizures that can significantly disrupt daily routines, interpersonal connections, and work opportunities. Regrettably, epilepsy stands as one of the prevalent neurological disorders, magnifying the challenges faced by a substantial number of people in managing their health and well-being.

news.headline: Rise of InfoStealer malware poses a threat to digital finance; over 2 million bank card details leaked last year. User vigilance is crucial to prevent attacks like these.
news.summary: Jimmy Su, Binance's chief security officer, warns about the increasing threat of InfoStealer malware, which is designed to extract sensitive data from devices without the user's knowledge. Kaspersky reported over 2 million leaked bank card details due to InfoStealers in the last year. Malware-as-a-service allows cybercriminals to easily access these tools. Binance has seen a rise in users affected by InfoStealer infections recently. These malware are distributed through phishing emails, fake downloads, malicious browser extensions, and compromised websites. Signs of infection include unusual browser notifications, unauthorized logins, and system slowdowns. Various InfoStealer malware variants are targeting Windows and macOS users, such as RedLine, LummaC2, Vidar, and AsyncRAT for Windows, and Atomic Stealer, Poseidon, and Banshee for macOS. Binance advises users to use antivirus tools, enable two-factor authentication, avoid saving passwords in browsers, and keep devices updated to protect against InfoStealers. The article emphasizes the importance of user education and cyber hygiene to prevent cyber threats.

news.headline: SC bill suggests making Bitcoin part of state reserve with up to 10% of public funds. Rep. Pace leads the proposal.
news.summary: State Representative Jordan Pace has suggested allocating a portion of specific public funds, potentially up to 10%, towards digital assets such as Bitcoin. This proposal aims to diversify the investment portfolio of the state and could involve a significant amount of money being transferred into the digital asset sector. Pace's idea may lead to a substantial increase in Bitcoin holdings within the state's assets, potentially resulting in a notable impact on the overall financial strategy and risk exposure of the funds involved.

news.headline: Particles for Humanity and Hexagon Nutrition partner to combat vitamin A deficiency in Africa. Hexagon Nutrition will manufacture supplements.
news.summary: A collaboration has been formed between Particles for Humanity and Hexagon Nutrition Limited to combat vitamin A deficiency (VAD) in Africa. The partnership involves Hexagon Nutrition obtaining the rights to produce vital supplements. This initiative aims to tackle the prevalent issue of VAD in the region. The venture signifies a crucial step towards providing essential nutrition to combat health challenges. The collaboration between the organizations is expected to significantly impact the fight against VAD in Africa, where a large number of individuals are affected by this deficiency. The joint effort by the two entities highlights their commitment to addressing health issues and underscores the importance of partnerships in enhancing healthcare accessibility.

news.headline: NFT sales plummet 63% in Q1 2025 to $1.5B, but Pudgy Penguins, Doodles shine. CryptoPunks sales at $60m, BAYC down to $29.8m. Bitcoin NFT average price up to $633.24. Bitcoin NFT sales drop 79% to $291m in Q1 2025.
news.summary: Sales of non-fungible tokens (NFTs) took a sharp dive in the first quarter of 2025, plunging 63% year-over-year to $1.5 billion from $4.1 billion in 2024 according to CryptoSlam. Notably, while big players like CryptoPunks and Bored Ape Yacht Club saw significant drops in sales, collections like Doodles, Milady Maker, and Pudgy Penguins stood out with impressive gains amid the downturn. Pudgy Penguins led the way with $72 million in sales, a 13% increase from the previous year, while Doodles saw a jump to $32 million. Milady Maker had the biggest percentage increase at 58%. Additionally, Bitcoin NFTs saw a rise in average price to $633.24 despite a 79% drop in sales volume to $291 million in 2025 from $1.4 billion in 2024. This marks a significant shift in the NFT market landscape.

news.headline: Signet Bank initiated Šiaulių Bankas coverage with target price EUR 1.27 - 35% upside from current valuation EUR 0.94.

news.headline: SBC Medical Group Holdings Inc. reported $44 million in total revenues for Q4 2024, a 29% decrease from the previous year.

SBC Medical is a worldwide firm that owns, operates, and offers management services and products to cosmetic treatment facilities.

news.headline: Workplace wellness is vital for long-term success, replacing traditional leadership styles. Article on Investor's Business Daily.
news.summary: A shift in leadership style has moved away from the traditional authoritarian approach to one that prioritizes employee well-being. In the workplace, focusing on wellness can lead to healthier and more sustainable success. This change is reflected in a recent article titled "Wellness At Work Unleashes Healthier, Long-Term Success." The piece emphasizes the importance of creating a work environment that values the physical and mental health of employees. This approach has shown to result in increased productivity and overall success for businesses. Embracing wellness initiatives can ultimately lead to better outcomes in the long run.
